title: Two Parties

jokes.text: Water? Like fire water?
jokes.setup: Ken is offered water in what appears to be a formal meeting
jokes.punchline: He immediately jumps to 'fire water' (alcohol stereotype about Native Americans)
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment that sets up Ken's personality - he deliberately invokes stereotypes to make people uncomfortable

jokes.text: Oh, my God! He's funny. This guy's funny.
jokes.setup: The revelation that Ken was joking
jokes.punchline: Over-enthusiastic relief and nervous laughter
jokes.explanation: Natural reaction beat that shows how uncomfortable the character was

jokes.text: But seriously. I'll have a whiskey.
jokes.setup: After the whole fire water bit
jokes.punchline: He actually does want alcohol, making the original question not entirely off-base
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that adds another layer to the opening bit - he does want alcohol after all

jokes.text: Murder-y.
jokes.setup: Describing the historical relationship between Pawnee and the Wamapoke
jokes.punchline: Understatement of genocide as 'murder-y'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - making genocide sound casual and folksy is peak Leslie Knope

jokes.text: Our slots are downright filthy.
jokes.setup: Describing a casino slogan
jokes.punchline: Double entendre about slot machines and sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Straightforward sexual double entendre - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Why don't we retire to my longhouse and smoke the ceremonial peace pipe to celebrate?
jokes.setup: Ken suggesting they celebrate the deal
jokes.punchline: Uses stereotypical Native American imagery
jokes.explanation: Ken continues his pattern of deliberately invoking Native American stereotypes

jokes.text: Is he joking? / I don't know.
jokes.setup: Ken's longhouse comment
jokes.punchline: Characters' genuine uncertainty about whether he's serious
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing how Ken keeps everyone off-balance

jokes.text: anything that can be penis-shaped will be penis-shaped
jokes.setup: Describing bachelorette party plans
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous extent of penis-themed party decorations
jokes.explanation: Standard bachelorette party joke - not particularly creative but gets the point across

jokes.text: Which is why I'm stress-eating gummy penises.
jokes.setup: Ann explaining how important the bachelorette party is
jokes.punchline: She's literally eating penis-shaped candy while talking about it
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy that reinforces Ann's anxiety while being absurd

jokes.text: The game is Settlers of Catan.
jokes.setup: Ben describing his bachelor party plans after building up expectations of craziness
jokes.punchline: It's just a board game
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - completely deflates expectations in a very Ben way

jokes.text: I mean, I'm nationally ranked, so it isn't even really fair.
jokes.setup: Ben apologetically offering to play something else
jokes.punchline: He's actually really good at this nerdy game
jokes.explanation: Great character detail that shows Ben's nerdiness extends to competitive board gaming

jokes.text: Is this gonna be one of those cool bachelorette parties where things get out of control and we murder someone, and then we all have to take a blood oath to never reveal our secret?
jokes.setup: April asking about the bachelorette party
jokes.punchline: Escalates from 'cool party' to murder and blood oaths
jokes.explanation: Classic April - takes innocent question to dark, absurd extremes in character-perfect way

jokes.text: Then I might have to leave early.
jokes.setup: Learning there won't be murder at the party
jokes.punchline: April's disappointment is so deadpan it's almost missed
jokes.explanation: Perfect April follow-up - her disappointment about no murder is played completely straight

jokes.text: Don't you ladies look nice... For once?
jokes.setup: Jamm trying to compliment the women
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermines it with a backhanded insult
jokes.explanation: Typical Jamm - tries to be charming but immediately ruins it

jokes.text: I've actually always been attracted to this one.
jokes.setup: Jamm qualifying his previous comment
jokes.punchline: Makes it even more inappropriate by singling someone out
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe moment showing Jamm's inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: Start drooling, fatties
jokes.setup: Reading the Paunch Burger sign
jokes.punchline: Insulting potential customers on the sign itself
jokes.explanation: Shows Jamm's complete lack of marketing sense and general awfulness

jokes.text: Their burgers will kill you! They're full of wasps!
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to discourage someone from wanting Paunch Burger
jokes.punchline: Escalates from unhealthy to literally filled with insects
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie moment - her opposition to junk food leads to absurd claims

jokes.text: Yeah, but they taste real good.
jokes.setup: Leslie's warning about wasp-filled burgers
jokes.punchline: Complete indifference to the health concerns
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pawnee citizen response - prioritizes taste over literally eating wasps

jokes.text: I'm still technically married, but my wife knows the deal.
jokes.setup: Jamm hitting on women
jokes.punchline: Admits to an open marriage arrangement
jokes.explanation: More Jamm inappropriateness - oversharing about his marriage

jokes.text: Seven contiguous segments, giving me longest road and a sweet-ass ten points for the win.
jokes.setup: Ben playing Catan
jokes.punchline: Gets extremely excited about board game terminology
jokes.explanation: Shows Ben's genuine passion for board games with specific terminology

jokes.text: I know you said no strippers 'cause they make you sad.
jokes.setup: Tom trying to plan alternative bachelor party activities
jokes.punchline: Ben's specific reason for not wanting strippers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben character detail - not morally opposed, just finds them depressing

jokes.text: Son, there's no wrong way to consume alcohol.
jokes.setup: Tom describing experimental alcohol consumption
jokes.punchline: Ron's practical approach to drinking
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron wisdom - simple and practical about alcohol

jokes.text: I ordered a beer. / That is a beer.
jokes.setup: Ben ordering a normal beer at the molecular mixology bar
jokes.punchline: The beer is served in some ridiculous molecular form
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about pretentious bar culture

jokes.text: This is the wrong way to consume alcohol.
jokes.setup: Ron's earlier statement about no wrong way to drink
jokes.punchline: He immediately finds an exception
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows even Ron has limits to his alcohol philosophy

jokes.text: It's a whiskey-infused lotion.
jokes.setup: Someone ordering Scotch at this molecular bar
jokes.punchline: It's served as lotion you rub on your hands
jokes.explanation: Peak absurdist molecular mixology - drinking through your skin

jokes.text: Can I ask if this entire establishment is a practical joke of some kind?
jokes.setup: The increasingly ridiculous drink service
jokes.punchline: Ben's logical conclusion that it must be a prank
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction to the absurdity

jokes.text: they have a vodka that's served in the form of a flash of light
jokes.setup: Offering alternatives to the lotion Scotch
jokes.punchline: An even more ridiculous way to 'serve' alcohol
jokes.explanation: Peak absurdity - alcohol as light itself

jokes.text: I have a penis on my head.
jokes.setup: Leslie at the bachelorette party
jokes.punchline: She's wearing penis-themed party gear
jokes.explanation: Predictable bachelorette party visual gag

jokes.text: This is why I prefer nightmares.
jokes.setup: Leslie talking about her dream being dead
jokes.punchline: April prefers nightmares to disappointed dreams
jokes.explanation: Perfect April philosophy - dark but logically consistent

jokes.text: Oh, this is bad. I should not have done this.
jokes.setup: Leslie realizing the implications of her artifact planting
jokes.punchline: Immediate regret after doing something clearly unethical
jokes.explanation: Leslie's quick moral compass kicking in

jokes.text: Well, if it isn't Babe Lincoln!
jokes.setup: Abraham Lincoln stripper arriving
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Babe' and Lincoln
jokes.explanation: Simple pun that works for the context

jokes.text: Emancipate them abs.
jokes.setup: Abraham Lincoln stripper about to perform
jokes.punchline: Pun on Emancipation Proclamation and abs
jokes.explanation: Clever historical pun that works well with the situation

jokes.text: I know for a fact this is a sex dream you've had.
jokes.setup: Ann encouraging Leslie to enjoy the Lincoln stripper
jokes.punchline: Assumes Leslie has had Lincoln sex dreams
jokes.explanation: Good character insight about Leslie's historical obsessions extending to fantasies

jokes.text: Looks like it's time for the Gettysburg undressed!
jokes.setup: Lincoln stripper about to undress
jokes.punchline: Pun on Gettysburg Address
jokes.explanation: Solid historical pun for a Lincoln stripper

jokes.text: I sat behind you in middle school band. Do you still play the clarinet?
jokes.setup: April recognizing the Lincoln stripper
jokes.punchline: Casual conversation with someone who was just stripping
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - treating an awkward situation as completely normal

jokes.text: No, it was a green card wedding.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining why he never had a bachelor party before marrying Wendy
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was a fake marriage for citizenship
jokes.explanation: Good character backstory reveal that explains Tom's romantic history

jokes.text: I didn't even have time to take a shower before my wedding. Or after. For, like, a week and a half.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his rushed wedding
jokes.punchline: Didn't shower for over a week including the wedding
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy gross-out humor that escalates believably

jokes.text: Tammy Two thwarted me by calling in a bomb threat to the steakhouse.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining why he never had a bachelor party
jokes.punchline: Tammy Two's extreme sabotage method
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing how destructive Tammy Two was

jokes.text: Boop! That's me hitting the snooze button. Don't talk again for another ten minutes.
jokes.setup: Jerry starting to tell his bachelor party story
jokes.punchline: Tom literally treating Jerry like an alarm clock
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about how boring Jerry is, treated like literal alarm clock

jokes.text: and to my bride, Rihanna.
jokes.setup: Tom toasting at his fantasy bachelor party
jokes.punchline: His imaginary bride is a famous pop star
jokes.explanation: Typical Tom fantasy about celebrity relationships

jokes.text: Shut up, Glenn.
jokes.setup: Glenn/Lincoln stripper trying to contribute to the conversation
jokes.punchline: Ann dismissively tells the stripper to be quiet
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ann's no-nonsense attitude

jokes.text: Use the penises.
jokes.setup: Leslie encouraging everyone to dig with the penis party favors
jokes.punchline: Treating penis decorations as actual tools
jokes.explanation: Good callback using the penis theme functionally

jokes.text: She was slender. Blonde hair, big breasts, long legs. Ugh, not my type at all.
jokes.setup: Jerry describing how he met his wife
jokes.punchline: Lists conventionally attractive features then claims they're not his type
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Jerry's unusual taste (or self-deprecation)

jokes.text: Was she ill, or did your father witness her father committing a crime? Or was she temporarily blind--
jokes.setup: Tom trying to understand how Jerry got Gayle
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly ridiculous explanations
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of increasingly desperate explanations for Jerry's success

jokes.text: No worries, Gary.
jokes.setup: Jerry dropping his ice cream cone
jokes.punchline: Sherm calls him by the wrong name
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry name confusion running gag

jokes.text: I always make a backup cone. He drops it about half the time.
jokes.setup: Sherm quickly replacing Jerry's dropped cone
jokes.punchline: He's prepared for Jerry's clumsiness
jokes.explanation: Great detail showing everyone expects Jerry to mess up

jokes.text: I want to see the Colts beat the Patriots 49 to nothing, then I yell at Tom Brady and make him cry, then I hook up with Dave Matthews, and we play golden tee for ten hours.
jokes.setup: Andy describing his impossible bachelor party dream
jokes.punchline: Escalates from sports to making Tom Brady cry to hooking up with Dave Matthews
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy fantasy - specific, escalating, and completely random

jokes.text: I'm dead. I am dead. And this is what heaven is like. I knew it.
jokes.setup: Andy seeing the Colts facility
jokes.punchline: Thinks he's literally died and gone to heaven
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy overreaction to his dream scenario

jokes.text: I can't tell if you're joking, but I hope you're not.
jokes.setup: Jim Irsay saying Andy's big enough to play football
jokes.punchline: Andy's genuine hope that he could play professional football
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his childlike optimism

jokes.text: I got married in your jersey. That's no joke.
jokes.setup: Andy meeting Reggie Wayne
jokes.punchline: Wore a football jersey to his wedding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy detail about his unconventional wedding attire

jokes.text: That's weird. But I respect that.
jokes.setup: Andy wearing his jersey to his wedding
jokes.punchline: Professional athlete's diplomatic response to fan weirdness
jokes.explanation: Good reaction showing how athletes handle weird fans

jokes.text: Donna just ripped off a stripper's underwear with her teeth.
jokes.setup: Ann continuing to lie about their exciting party
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up scenario about Donna
jokes.explanation: Absurd lie that's completely out of character for their actual situation

jokes.text: Can I take a break? My hands are tired.
jokes.setup: The group digging for artifacts
jokes.punchline: The Lincoln stripper is now doing manual labor
jokes.explanation: Good visual of the stripper now being used as manual labor

jokes.text: Gingrich, Gergich. I wonder if we're related.
jokes.setup: Jerry hearing the wrong name for their dinner reservation
jokes.punchline: Genuinely thinks he might be related to Newt Gingrich
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry naivety about name similarities

jokes.text: You'd be surprised.
jokes.setup: Bartender saying he doesn't need to specify liquid form for whiskey
jokes.punchline: Ron's experience with the molecular mixology bar
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Ron's traumatic experience at the fancy bar

jokes.text: I like her. Whoa! Wow!
jokes.setup: Ron being asked about Diane
jokes.punchline: Everyone shocked by Ron admitting feelings
jokes.explanation: Great character moment for emotionally guarded Ron to admit feelings.

jokes.text: I like her.
jokes.setup: The guys asking Ron about his relationship with Diane
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of Ron's feelings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron emotional expression - maximum restraint for major feelings

jokes.text: you can make wine in your toilet and fight people, which is a super-cool way to live.
jokes.setup: April describing prison life to comfort Leslie
jokes.punchline: Makes prison sound appealing
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - makes prison sound like a lifestyle choice

jokes.text: Japanese, Muslim, Jewish, none of those weird countries.
jokes.setup: Worker listing types of artifacts they haven't found
jokes.punchline: Calls religions 'weird countries'
jokes.explanation: Shows the worker's ignorance about religions vs. countries

jokes.text: I also found some weird-looking gummy candy.
jokes.setup: Finding artifacts at construction site
jokes.punchline: Discovers the gummy penises from bachelorette party
jokes.explanation: Good callback connecting the two party storylines through physical evidence.

jokes.text: You have turned into a very beautiful, wise, and fertile government employee.
jokes.setup: Leslie complimenting April
jokes.punchline: Adds 'fertile' to the list of compliments
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie over-compliment with inappropriate workplace comment

jokes.text: Don't try to bond with me.
jokes.setup: Ann trying to commiserate about Leslie's compliments
jokes.punchline: April shuts down any attempt at bonding
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - rejects human connection immediately

jokes.text: Dance or smell like a native American.
jokes.setup: Leslie listing historical Pawnee discrimination
jokes.punchline: Illegal to smell like a Native American
jokes.explanation: Absurdly specific historical discrimination that sounds made up but fits the show

jokes.text: I wanted to raise the bar of awfulness so that when I confess something to you, you have perspective.
jokes.setup: Leslie listing historical atrocities
jokes.punchline: Strategic context-setting for her confession
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leslie strategy - manipulating the moral curve before confessing

jokes.text: Full disclosure-- I voted for Ryan Gosling, but you came in a close second.
jokes.setup: Tom giving Chris an award for Best Nan
jokes.punchline: Admits he voted for Ryan Gosling for best non-married guy
jokes.explanation: Typical Tom celebrity obsession affecting his judgment

jokes.text: Maybe it's April. Maybe I die. Skydiving explosion. Pbbbt.
jokes.setup: Tom imagining Chris's future wife
jokes.punchline: Imagines his own death by skydiving explosion
jokes.explanation: Tom's dramatic imagination about unlikely death scenarios

jokes.text: In a cave. Training.
jokes.setup: Tom describing his life after Chris 'steals' April
jokes.punchline: Living like a revenge movie character
jokes.explanation: Escalates Tom's revenge fantasy to action movie levels

jokes.text: He really plays white people like a fiddle.
jokes.setup: Ken manipulating the situation with cultural references
jokes.punchline: April's blunt assessment of Ken's manipulation tactics
jokes.explanation: Perfect April observation about Ken's strategic use of white guilt

jokes.text: Why, yes, I-- I thought that was obvious.
jokes.setup: Jamm asking if Ken's casino threat was a threat
jokes.punchline: Ken's matter-of-fact confirmation
jokes.explanation: Ken's casual confirmation of what was obviously a threat

jokes.text: In the spirit of fair play, I suggest we put on these authentic Wamapoke headdresses and dance around the table.
jokes.setup: Ken offering a ceremonial gesture
jokes.punchline: Suggests stereotypical Native American imagery
jokes.explanation: Ken continues his pattern of suggesting stereotypical activities

jokes.text: Does it, white man?
jokes.setup: Jamm saying the headdress dance sounds offensive
jokes.punchline: Ken questions his authority to determine what's offensive
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal - makes Jamm question his own cultural sensitivity

jokes.text: I have thousands of seconds.
jokes.setup: Shauna asking if Chris has a second for lunch
jokes.punchline: Chris's literal, enthusiastic response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris response - literally interprets time measurement with enthusiasm

tmdb_overview: Ben and Leslie's rival bachelor and bachelorette parties each take on a very different tune.
insight: Political satire crashes two party formats into 72 jokes across dueling campaign events.
